dsl_rule.translation_notes: Bullish Belt Hold pattern translated as: (1) open near low of day, (2) close above open (white candle), (3) tall body (body_pct > 0.7 represents tall white candle), (4) close near high coupled with support above sma_50. Exit when price falls below 50-day moving average indicating loss of support/uptrend. Pattern optimally occurs during retracement in uptrend, captured by target_regime TRENDING_UP. Underlying support condition represented by proximity to sma_50.

backtest_rule.reason: Bullish Belt Hold pattern translated as: (1) open near low of day, (2) close above open (white candle), (3) tall body (body_pct > 0.7 represents tall white candle), (4) close near high coupled with support above sma_50. Exit when price falls below 50-day moving average indicating loss of support/uptrend. Pattern optimally occurs during retracement in uptrend, captured by target_regime TRENDING_UP. Underlying support condition represented by proximity to sma_50.

source_rule_text: Bullish Belt Hold: A candlestick pattern where price opens near the low for the day and climbs, forming a tall white candle when price closes near the high. Works best as part of a retracement in an uptrend and marks a reliable turning point when coupled with underlying support.
